creator: dym@afalina.od.ua
text:
I agree with Daniel Engberg.

Add:
.for opt in ${OPTIONS_UNSET}
FLAVOR?=	${FLAVORS:[1]}
. if  ${opt} == "X11"
    OPTIONS_DEFAULT=	JSON
    FLAVOR=		nox11
. else
    OPTIONS_DEFAULT=	JSON X11
. endif
.endfor

Remove:
OPTIONS_DEFAULT=JSON
The contents of this variable are selected dynamically by the previous code.

Add:
.if empty(PORT_OPTIONS:MX11)
    FLAVOR=		nox11
.endif
This code allows you to choose how to build a package directly from the build configuration menu. And this value is saved in a standard method in the build settings database.

Also, the lines that allow you to correctly display the build options selection have been moved to a more convenient place.
Note that this is not a change of the port code, as the FLAVOR itself is changed and the logic of operation is preserved.
These few small changes will make life easier for many port builders without destroying the FLAVOR logic.

The rest of the lines contain only cosmetic indentation additions to make it easier to read.
These changes keep it FreeBSD-style and are consistent with all other ports (except ImageMagick and maybe a few others, I don't keep track of all the ports).
You should not break the default behavior of make.conf and make config commands, it is a bad practice.
Just because it is more Linux-style does not mean it should be copied.

I might also look into making a Makefile for ImageMagick to preserve the functionality and standard methods for FreeBSD, and FLAVOR.
